message:
chore(ft-xbnl0.2.4): portable mktemp in check script (tick 370)

BSD `mktemp -t PREFIX` and GNU `mktemp -t TEMPLATE` have different
semantics for the argument after `-t`:

- macOS / BSD: `mktemp -t ft-xbnl.XXXXXX` → creates a file in $TMPDIR
  with "ft-xbnl.XXXXXX" as the filename prefix (and the trailing
  `.XXXXXX` replaced with random chars).
- Linux / GNU: `mktemp -t TEMPLATE` treats TEMPLATE as a filename
  template (must end with X's), but in some older coreutils versions
  the `-t` flag was deprecated or interpreted differently.

Portable form that both accept unambiguously is the positional
argument: `mktemp <full-template-path>`. Updated to:

    tmp="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/ft-xbnl0-2-4-check.XXXXXX")"

Uses $TMPDIR when set (respects macOS's per-user TMPDIR and CI runner
env), falls back to /tmp. Full template path avoids the `-t` ambiguity.

Added comment explaining the portability concern so a future editor
doesn't regress this.

Verified: script still runs cleanly locally, 76/76 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -64,7 +64,10 @@ run_test() {
     # Capture stdout + stderr to a tempfile so we can tally the "N passed"
     # count while still showing the output to the operator.
     local tmp
-    tmp="$(mktemp -t ft-xbnl0-2-4-check.XXXXXX)"
+    # Explicit template path for portability — BSD (`mktemp -t PREFIX`)
+    # and GNU (`mktemp -t TEMPLATE`) differ on the `-t` argument form,
+    # but both accept a full template path as a positional argument.
+    tmp="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/ft-xbnl0-2-4-check.XXXXXX")"
     if cargo test "$@" 2>&1 | tee "${tmp}"; then
         # Sum all "N passed" counts from all `test result: ok.` lines in
         # this run (per-binary splits may produce multiple result lines
